passive.name: Echoing Flames
passive.description: Urgot's basic attacks and Purge periodically trigger blasts of flame from his legs, dealing physical damage.

wiki_detail.blurb: {{sbc|Innate:}} {{cis|Urgot}} six legs each conceal a shotgun that covers an arc, together forming a circle around him. Each shotgun can fire once before incurring a unique {{sti|cooldown}}.
wiki_detail.blurb2: '''Urgot's''' next [[basic attack]] within the arc of a shotgun will cause it to fire in a cone, dealing {{as|physical damage}} to enemies hit based on {{as|their '''maximum''' health}}.
wiki_detail.description: {{sbc|Innate:}} '''Urgot's''' six legs each conceal a shotgun that covers an arc, together forming a full circle of cover around him. Each shotgun can fire once before incurring a unique {{sti|cooldown}}.
wiki_detail.description2: '''Urgot's''' next [[basic attack]] {{tip|on-hit}} within the arc of a shotgun will cause it to fire in a cone, dealing {{as|{{pp|key=%|40 to 100 for 6|1;6;9;11;13;15}} AD}} {{as|(+ {{pp|changedisplay=true|key=%|2 to 6 for 5|1;6;9;11;13}} of target's '''maximum''' health)}} {{as|physical damage}} to enemies hit, capped at {{pp|100 to 360}} against {{tip|monsters}}.
wiki_detail.effect radius: {{tip|cr|icononly = true}} {{tt|700|Effect range, estimated}}<!-- / {{tt|X|Trigger range, pending for test}}-->
wiki_detail.notes:
* ''Echoing Flames'':
** Shotgun attack range scales with '''bonus''' attack range.
** Will not trigger if the target is too far away or they are dead when the basic attack hits.
** Will not trigger while {{tip|Blind|blinded}} or if the basic attack is blocked by {{ai|Wind Wall|Yasuo}} or {{ai|Blade Whirl|Samira}}.
*** It bypasses {{tip|block|blocking}} and {{tip|dodge|dodging}} effects.
** Will not trigger against [[structures]] nor [[ward]]s.
** Will trigger from {{iis|Runaan's Hurricane}} additional bolts.
** Will not apply {{stil|life steal}}, but does apply on {{stil|spell vamp}} and [[omnivamp]] at 33% effectiveness.
** Will apply [[spell effect]]s.
** Will not trigger when basic attacking certain units (e.g {{cai|Jack in the Box|Shaco}}), however it will trigger when attacking them with {{ai|Purge|Urgot}}.
** Will consume {{ri|Manaflow Band}} if it is available.
** Will not consume {{ii|Tear of the Goddess}} charges. {{bug|1}}
* '''Urgot's''' legs never change in orientation, with his body instead rotating on top of them; the leg that faces northeast will always face northeast. This does not affect '''Urgot's''' facing direction for the purposes of abilities such as {{ai|Petrifying Gaze|Cassiopeia}}.

Q.name: Corrosive Charge
Q.description: Fires an explosive charge at the target location, dealing physical damage and slowing enemies caught in the explosion.
Q.tooltip: Urgot fires an explosive charge, dealing {{ totaldamage }} physical damage and Slowing by {{ slowamount*100 }}% for {{ slowduration }} seconds.{{ spellmodifierdescriptionappend }}

wiki_detail.blurb: {{sbc|Active:}} {{ci|Urgot}} launches a canister at the target location that explodes after a brief delay, dealing {{as|physical damage}} and briefly {{tip|slow|slowing}} enemies hit.
wiki_detail.description: {{sbc|Active:}} '''Urgot''' launches a canister at the target location. Upon landing, it explodes after {{fd|0.3}} seconds to deal {{as|physical damage}} to enemies hit and {{tip|slow}} them for {{fd|1.25}} seconds.
wiki_detail.notes: * ''Corrosive Charge'' prevents {{ai|Purge|Urgot}} from firing for {{fd|0.3}} seconds.

W.name: Purge
W.description: Urgot slows himself while he unloads his weapon on nearby enemies. Prioritizes enemy champions Urgot has recently struck with other abilities and triggers Echoing Flames.

wiki_detail.blurb: {{sbc|Passive:}} {{cis|Urgot}} other abilities ''mark'' enemy {{tip|champions}} hit for a few seconds, only one enemy can be ''marked'' at a time. At max rank, ''Purge'' lasts indefinitely and becomes a {{sbc|toggled}} ability.
wiki_detail.blurb2: {{sbc|Active:}} For a few seconds, '''Urgot''' becomes {{tip|ghosted}} and autonomously attacks the nearest enemy at a {{sti|as|{{as|'''fixed''' attack speed}}}}, prioritizing the ''marked'' enemy. ''Purge'' can be recast within the duration, and does so automatically afterwards.
wiki_detail.blurb4: ''Purge'' attacks deal {{as|'''modified''' physical damage}} and cannot {{tip|critically strike}}. '''Urgot''' gains {{tip|slow resist}} and can move while firing, but his {{as|movement speed}} is reduced.
wiki_detail.description:
{{sbc|Passive:}} '''Urgot's''' other abilities ''mark'' enemy {{tip|champions}} hit for 5 seconds. Only one enemy can be ''marked'' at a time.

Additionally, at maximum rank, ''Purge'' lasts indefinitely and becomes a toggled ability.
wiki_detail.description2: {{sbc|Active:}} '''Urgot''' equips his machine gun for 4 seconds, granting {{tip|ghosting}} to minions and non-epic monsters around him and autonomously firing at the nearest enemy at a '''fixed''' {{sti|attack speed|{{as|{{fd|3.0}} attack speed}}}}, prioritizing ''marked'' enemy champions and refreshing the ''mark'' with every attack. While firing, '''Urgot''' is able to move and gains 40% {{tip|slow resist}}, but his {{as|'''base''' movement speed}} is reduced by {{as|125|ms}}.
wiki_detail.description3: Attacks with ''Purge'' deal {{as|'''modified''' physical damage}}, with a minimum threshold of 50 against {{tip|monsters}} and {{tip|minions}}, and apply {{tip|on-hit}} and {{tip|on-attack}} damage at 50% effectiveness, but they cannot {{tip|critically strike}}. '''Urgot''' cannot perform attacks while unable to declare basic attacks.
wiki_detail.description4: ''Purge'' can be recast after {{fd|0.5}} seconds within the duration, and does so automatically afterwards.
wiki_detail.effect radius: {{tip|er|icononly = true}} {{tt|490|Center-to-edge; See notes for more details}}
wiki_detail.notes:
* Autonomous attack range scales with '''bonus''' attack range.
* ''Purge'' uses {{tip|edge range}} for enemy targets only; Its range is ''center-to-edge''.
**'''Urgot's''' [[size]] increasing does not increase its range.
* The initial cast and the manual recast count as ability activations for the purposes of on-cast effects such as {{tip|Spellblade}} and triggering {{ais|Force Pulse|Kassadin}} passive.
** Before maximum rank, if ''Purge'' is not manually recasted, the effect will end without being considered as an ability activation.
* '''Urgot's''' facing direction during ''Purge'' is in the direction he's moving and not in the direction of his attack target.
* Before maximum rank, if continuously attacking without being interrupted or interrupting or canceling the cast, '''Urgot''' will attack 13 times.
* The bolts are considered basic attacks and thus apply {{sti|life steal|link=true}} at full efficiency.
* During ''Purge'', '''Urgot's''' {{sti|attack range|link=range}} is reduced by 200 and his attack commands instead issue movement commands to walk into the respective range of his target.
* {{tip|Slow resist}} does not modify flat reductions in movement speed.
* Percentage {{tip|slow|slows}} apply after the flat movement reduction.
* ''Purge's'' attacks do not interact with '''Urgot's''' {{tip|basic attack}} timer.
** After ending ''Purge'', he can usually attack again immediately.
*  If '''Urgot''' is {{tip|berserk|berserked}} or {{tip|taunt|taunted}}, he will fire at the unit he is forced to attack.
* ''Purge'' will fire while '''Urgot''' is {{tip|dash|dashing}} (e.g. while taking {{cai|Dark Passage|Thresh}}), with the exception of {{ais|Disdain|Urgot}} dash.
* Each shot counts as a separate hit for effects such as {{ri|Electrocute}}, {{iis|Muramana}} {{nie|Shock}}, and {{iis|Eclipse}} {{nie|Ever Rising Moon}}.
* ''Purge'' will apply {{ii|Runaan's Hurricane}} on every shot at 100% of the listed damage.
** On-hit damage applied by the bolts are still reduced by ''Purge''.
* The movement speed stat tooltip erroneously reads that the '''bonus''' movement speed is being reduced before '''base''' movement speed.{{bug|2}}

E.name: Disdain
E.description: Urgot charges in a direction, shielding himself and trampling non-champion enemies. If he catches an enemy champion, he will stop and hurl them out of his way.
E.tooltip: Urgot charges forward, gaining {{ etotalshieldhealth }} Shield for {{ eshieldduration }} seconds. The first champion hit is Stunned for {{ stunduration }} seconds and thrown behind Urgot. All enemies Urgot collides with take {{ edamage }} physical damage.{{ spellmodifierdescriptionappend }}

wiki_detail.blurb: {{sbc|Active:}} {{ci|Urgot}} {{tip|shield|shields}} himself for a few seconds and {{tip|dashes}} in the target direction, dealing {{as|physical damage}} and briefly {{tip|stun|stunning}} enemies hit, {{tip|airborne|knocking them aside}}.
wiki_detail.blurb2: '''Urgot''' will stop upon hitting an enemy {{tip|champion}}, briefly {{tip|stun|stunning}} and {{tip|airborne|flinging}} them over him.
wiki_detail.description2: After the cast time, '''Urgot''' {{tip|dashes}} in the target direction, though not through terrain, dealing {{as|physical damage}} to enemies he passes through, {{tip|airborne|knocking them aside}} and {{tip|stun|stunning}} them for 1 second.
wiki_detail.description3: '''Urgot''' will stop upon hitting an enemy {{tip|champion}}, {{tip|stun|stunning}} them for {{fd|1.5}} seconds and, after {{fd|0.25}} seconds, also {{tip|airborne|flinging}} them to the location {{tt|100 units behind him|this destination is a fixed location and is unaffected by size modifiers on Urgot or the target moving away before the displacement started}}, though not through terrain.
wiki_detail.description4: ''{{ai|Purge|Urgot}} can be cast during the dash.''
wiki_detail.notes:
* '''Urgot''' turns around when tossing an enemy over him, always facing his victim.
* The target will be {{tip|airborne|flung}} to the location behind '''Urgot''' over {{fd|0.5}} seconds given that they do not move nor are moved from the position at which they collided with him while {{tip|stun|stunned}}.
* ''Disdain'' prevents {{ai|Purge|Urgot}} from firing for {{fd|1.25}} seconds.
* The range indicator length is 475 units, but the dash only moves '''Urgot''' 450 units.
{{Effect at cast time end}}
* The {{tip|shield}} is granted at the start of the cast time while the {{tip|dash}} begins after.
* The {{tip|airborne|fling}} will occur after {{fd|0.25}} seconds regardless of how far away the target is.
* {{tip|Displacement immunity}} will not resist the application of the {{tip|stun}}.

R.name: Fear Beyond Death
R.description: Urgot fires a chem-drill that impales the first enemy champion hit. If that champion falls below a health threshold, Urgot judges them weak and can execute them.
R.tooltip:
Urgot fires a chem-drill, impaling the first champion hit, dealing {{ rcalculateddamage }} physical damage and Slowing for {{ rslowduration }} seconds by 1% per 1% missing Health, up to {{ rmovespeedmod }}%.

If the impaled victim falls below {{ rhealththreshold }}% Health, Urgot can Recast this Ability, Suppressing the victim and dragging them to himself. On reaching Urgot, they are killed and nearby enemies are Feared for {{ rfearduration }} seconds.{{ spellmodifierdescriptionappend }}

wiki_detail.blurb: {{sbc|Active:}} {{ci|Urgot}} fires a chem-drill in the target direction that impales the first enemy champion hit, dealing {{as|physical damage}} and leashing them for a few seconds. The target is also {{tip|true sight|revealed}} and {{tip|slow|slowed}} based on {{as|their '''missing''' health}}.
wiki_detail.blurb2: ''Fear Beyond Death'' can be recast if the leashed target falls to {{as|low health}}, and does so automatically afterwards if they remain at {{as|low health}}. It will also recast if the chem-drill would kill the target.
wiki_detail.blurb3: {{sbc|Recast:}} '''Urgot''' {{tip|suppression|suppresses}} the target and reels them in, {{tip|true sight|revealing}} them and rendering them {{tip|untargetable}}. He then {{tip|execute|executes}} them, which briefly {{tip|fear|fears}} and {{tip|slows}} nearby enemies.
wiki_detail.description: {{sbc|Active:}} '''Urgot''' fires a chem-drill in the target direction that impales the first enemy champion hit, dealing {{as|physical damage}} and leashing the target for 4 seconds, during which they are {{tip|standard sight|revealed}} and {{tip|slow|slowed}} by {{as|{{pp|type=target's '''missing''' health|key=%|0 to 75 for 11|key1=%|0 to 75|formula=1% per 1% missing health. ''This is capped at 75%''}}|health}}.
wiki_detail.description2: ''Fear Beyond Death'' can be recast if the leashed target is below {{as|{{sti|health|25% of their '''maximum''' health}}}}, and does so automatically if the chem-drill {{tt|would kill the target|Damage instance does not reduce their health below 1 in this case}}, the ability is cast again while the target was above the threshold and is then brought within it, or the target is within the threshold at the end of the duration.
wiki_detail.description3: {{sbc|Recast - Mercy:}} '''Urgot''' launches chains at the leashed target, then {{tip|channel|channels}} for {{fd|1.5}} seconds once they latch on. During this time, he reels them toward him, {{tip|suppression|suppressing}} them, {{tip|true sight|revealing}} them, rendering them {{tip|untargetable}}, and preventing them from taking damage from {{tt|other sources|Negates application of damage from any source that is not the execution}}. If the target is near '''Urgot''' when the chains connect, they are pushed up to {{tt|1000|Estimated}} units away based on their proximity to him. This channel cannot be {{tip|interrupt|interrupted}} by {{tip|crowd control}}.
wiki_detail.description4: Upon completing the channel, the target is {{ccs|{{tip|execute|executed}}|true}}. If the execution is successful, he {{tip|fear|fears}} nearby enemies for {{fd|1.5}} seconds, during which they are {{tip|slow|slowed}} by 75%.
wiki_detail.description5: ''Fear Beyond Death's recast can be used while affected by {{tip|cast-inhibiting crowd control}}.''
wiki_detail.cooldown: {{tt|{{ap|100 to 70}}|Starts on-cast if no target is hit, post-effect if it does}}
wiki_detail.notes:
* Unlike most [[Projectile|missiles]], ''Fear Beyond Death'' '''can''' hit the edge of targets whose center is further than the missile range.
** However, it still cannot hit targets whose center is behind '''Urgot'''.
* Applies {{tip|spell damage}} on first cast and {{tip|raw damage}} on recast.
* '''Urgot''' is {{tip|sight|revealed}} while the target is leashed and being reeled in.
* ''Fear Beyond Death's'' execute against the enemy champion will aggro nearby enemy minions.
* While ''Fear Beyond Death'' is not on cooldown, all enemy champions will have an execution indicator on their health bar, and a visual indicator if below the execution threshold.
* ''Mercy's'' granted {{tip|untargetability}} does not {{tip|Homing projectile destruction|destroy}} in-flight {{tip|projectile|projectiles}} for the target.
* '''Urgot''' will place himself onto the ground and interrupts {{tip|airborne|displacements}} affecting him upon starting the channel.
** If the displacement effect is a ''knock up'' with no debuff source, the ''airborne'' status is not removed and he spends significantly less time in the air.
{{Effect at cast time start}}
* ''Fear Beyond Death'' prevents {{ai|Purge|Urgot}} from firing for {{fd|0.8}} seconds.
** The recast prevents {{ai|Purge|Urgot}} from firing for {{fd|1.5}} seconds.
* If '''Urgot''' is in [[Channel#Casting Times|cast time]] or is {{tip|dash|dashing}} while the chains are in transit, ''Mercy's'' forced movement will be deferred (the debuff will continuously refresh) until the cast or dash is complete. The target will still be ''untargetable'', prevented from taking damage from other sources, and ''suppressed'' during this time.
** If '''Urgot''' is {{tip|channel|channeling}} a spell, the channel is interrupted when the chains hit.
* Removing or resisting the {{tip|slow}} has no effect on the mark [[debuff]].
* The initial cast always counts as an ability activation for the purposes of on-cast effects such as {{tip|Spellblade}} and triggering {{ais|Force Pulse|Kassadin}} passive.
** If the ability hits a target who is below the threshold, it will automatically recast itself and it won't be considered as an ability activation.
** If the ability hits a target who is above the threshold and it's recast early, the recast will happen automatically when they reach the threshold and it won't be considered as an ability activation.
** If the target got hit while above the threshold and it is recast after they fall below it, the recast will be considered as an ability activation.
* Reactivating the ability early will reel the target as soon as they reach the {{as|25% '''maximum''' health}} threshold.
** ''Mercy'' can be cast and will automatically do so at the end of the duration if the target is below the execute threshold regardless of '''Urgot''' being affected by {{tip|crowd control}}, including self or enemy-imposed {{tip|stasis}}.
* ''Fear Beyond Death'' can affect {{tip|clone|clones}} and activates its fear when executing one.
* ''Fear Beyond Death's'' recast cannot be used if the target is {{tip|untargetable}}.
* ''Fear Beyond Death's'' recast cannot be used during {{tip|resurrection}}. Attempting to do so places the recast on a 5-second {{sti|cooldown}}.
** If the target is below the threshold at the end of the duration and '''Urgot''' is revived, the recast will initiate regardless of this cooldown.
* The recast's chains are classified as a {{tip|projectile}} and they will fail to affect their target if:
** '''Urgot''' or his target {{tip|death|dies}} or enters {{tip|resurrection}}.
** The target becomes {{tip|untargetable}}, even if the granted source does not {{tip|homing projectile destruction|destroy}} in-flight projectiles.
*** '''Urgot''' will also fail to execute the target if they become {{tip|untargetable}} while the chains are being pulled.{{bug|2}}
** They collide with {{ai|Wind Wall|Yasuo}} or {{ai|Unbreakable|Braum}}.
*** If {{ai|Unbreakable|Braum}} intercepts the chains and {{ci|Braum}} is not the target, they're destroyed.
*** If {{ci|Braum}} is the target, he will still be reeled in and executed as normal.
** The target is {{tip|cc-immune|crowd control immune}} when the chains hit.
** The target is {{tip|zombie state|a zombie}}.
* {{tip|spell shield|Spell shield}} will '''not''' block the recast's chains.
* If the target removes the {{tip|suppression}} by any means they will prevent themselves from being {{tip|execute|executed}} and stop the forced movement from being renewed, though allowing the most recent one to be present and move them to '''Urgot's''' previous location.
** Their untargetability and damage prevention of other sources granted by ''Fear Beyond Death'' is removed in the process.
** '''Urgot''' will still complete the full channel despite this.
*** The execution would not count as being successful in this case and thus he will not {{tip|fear}} nearby enemies after the failed execution.
* If the target is {{tip|displacement immune}} when the chains collide, '''Urgot''' will not begin the channel until after their displacement immunity ends.
** The target will be unaffected by ''Fear Beyond Death's'' forced movement during this time, but will still be suppressed, prevented from taking damage from other sources and rendered untargetable.
** If the target's displacement immunity does not end within 10 seconds, ''Fear Beyond Death'' will cancel.
* When the chains start reeling in and suppressing a [[Summoner|player's]] champion, chains will also cover the sides of their screen.
* The target champion shrinks in [[size]] in several steps after reaching '''Urgot's''' grinder and before being executed.
** If the ''suppression'' ends in any way, the target returns to its normal size.
** If the execution is successful, the victim's corpse will be effectively invisible unless they enter a {{tip|zombie state}}.
* The target will die regardless of their {{as|'''current''' health}} once the recast's chains hit, even if they are {{tip|heal|healed}}.
** If the target has {{tip|invulnerability}} that persists for the duration of the channel, it will protect them from the execution.
*** {{ai|Undying Rage|Tryndamere}} will prevent {{ci|Tryndamere}} from dying by the execute. In this case, the execute will bypass his minimum health threshold though and reduce him to {{as|1 health}}.
*** The effect will also not prevent the activation of {{tip|resurrection}} effects, such as {{ii|Guardian Angel}} or {{cai|Rebirth|Anivia}}.
*** [[Death#Post-death_events|Post-death events]] such as {{tip|zombie state|Zombie State}} effects (e.g, {{cai|Death Defied|Karthus}}, {{cai|Icathian Surprise|Kog'Maw}}, and {{cai|Glory in Death|Sion}}) will be triggered.
*** Only the target's {{as|'''current''' health}} will count as '''Urgot's''' damage in post-game statistics and {{sti|spell vamp|link=true}} healing.
* ''Fear Beyond Death's'' initial cast will not perform the automatic recast from dealing enough damage to kill {{ci|Sion}}.
* ''Fear Beyond Death's'' recast will be placed on a 5-second {{sti|cooldown}} if the target is below the threshold and becomes targetable again after completing the dash from {{ai|Hero's Entrance|Galio}}.
* The following table refers for interactions while '''Urgot''' is {{tip|channel|channeling}}:
{{ct|channel|attack=false|cast=false|move=true|items=false, true|spells=true, true, false|interrupts=death}}
wiki_detail.flavorsound:
{{sm2|Urgot_Original_R_Hit_2.ogg}} ''"The end is near."''
{{sm2|Urgot Original R SFX execution threshold reached.ogg}} {{tt|''Execution threshold alert''.|This sound effect plays when the target's health drops below the execution threshold.}}
